expectations.text: Response has three sections: Architecture Overview, Code Implementation, Review Points
expectations.evidence: The response does not have these three named sections. Instead it has: a file structure overview, individual code sections per file (types, validation, hook, components), and a '핵심 설계 포인트' (Key Design Points) section. There is no explicit 'Architecture Overview', 'Code Implementation', or 'Review Points' section.

expectations.text: Types/Schemas, Hooks, and Components are separated into distinct files or clearly labeled sections
expectations.evidence: Types are in types.ts, validation logic in validation.ts, the hook in useMultiStepForm.ts, and components in separate .tsx files (StepIndicator, StepPersonalInfo, StepAddress, StepConfirmation, MultiStepForm). Each is presented as a clearly labeled section with file name in the heading.

expectations.text: No usage of 'any' type in TypeScript code
expectations.evidence: No usage of the 'any' type anywhere in the code. All types are explicitly defined: PersonalInfo, Address, FormData, ValidationErrors (using index signature { [key: string]: string }), and all component props have proper interfaces.

expectations.text: Uses shadcn/ui components (e.g., Form, Input, Button)
expectations.evidence: The response uses plain HTML elements (input, button, div) with Tailwind CSS classes. There are no imports or usage of shadcn/ui components such as Form, Input, Button, or any @/components/ui imports.

expectations.text: Each code block includes a file path comment
expectations.evidence: Code blocks do not include file path comments inside the code. The file paths appear only in the markdown headings above each code block (e.g., '## 타입 정의 (`types.ts`)'), but not as comments within the code blocks themselves (e.g., no '// src/components/MultiStepForm/types.ts' at the top of the code).

expectations.text: Uses Zod schemas for per-step validation
expectations.evidence: The response uses custom validation functions (validatePersonalInfo, validateAddress) with manual string checks and regex. There is no import or usage of Zod anywhere in the code.

expectations.text: State persists when navigating back to previous steps
expectations.evidence: The useMultiStepForm hook stores all form data in a single formData state object. The goBack function only changes currentStep without resetting formData. The design points section explicitly confirms: '단계 이동 시 currentStep만 변경하고, formData는 그대로 유지합니다' (Only currentStep changes during step navigation, formData is preserved).

expectations.text: Uses a Compound/Headless pattern or clear step abstraction for multi-step navigation
expectations.evidence: The implementation uses a custom hook (useMultiStepForm) that encapsulates all step navigation logic (goNext, goBack, goToStep, validateCurrentStep) separately from the UI. Each step is a separate component receiving data via props. The main MultiStepForm renders steps conditionally based on currentStep. This constitutes a clear step abstraction pattern with separation of logic and presentation.
